title: xylosma pachyphylla
intro: Xylosma pachyphylla, also known as the small-leaved xylosma, is a plant species native to Australia and New Guinea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, and it has been used to treat conditions such as rheumatism and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and other remedies, which can be taken internally or applied topically.
